class.name: FlatteningPathIterator
class.signature: public class FlatteningPathIterator extends Object implements PathIterator
class.description: The FlatteningPathIterator class returns a flattened view of another PathIterator object. Other Shape classes can use this class to provide flattening behavior for their paths without having to perform the interpolation calculations themselves.

constructors.name: FlatteningPathIterator
constructors.description: Constructs a new FlatteningPathIterator object that flattens a path as it iterates over it. The iterator does not subdivide any curve read from the source iterator to more than 10 levels of subdivision which yields a maximum of 1024 line segments per curve.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: the original unflattened path being iterated over

parameters.name: flatness
parameters.description: the maximum allowable distance between the control points and the flattened curve

constructors.name: FlatteningPathIterator
constructors.signature: public FlatteningPathIterator(PathIterator src, double flatness, int limit)
constructors.description: Constructs a new FlatteningPathIterator object that flattens a path as it iterates over it. The limit parameter allows you to control the maximum number of recursive subdivisions that the iterator can make before it assumes that the curve is flat enough without measuring against the flatness parameter. The flattened iteration therefore never generates more than a maximum of (2^limit) line segments per curve.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: the original unflattened path being iterated over

parameters.name: flatness
parameters.description: the maximum allowable distance between the control points and the flattened curve

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: the maximum number of recursive subdivisions allowed for any curved segment

throws.description: if flatness or limit is less than zero

methods.name: getFlatness
methods.description: Returns the flatness of this iterator.

methods.name: getRecursionLimit
methods.description: Returns the recursion limit of this iterator.
methods.returns: the recursion limit of this FlatteningPathIterator.

methods.name: getWindingRule
methods.description: Returns the winding rule for determining the interior of the path.
methods.returns: the winding rule of the original unflattened path being iterated over.

methods.name: isDone
methods.description: Tests if the iteration is complete.
methods.returns: true if all the segments have been read; false otherwise.

methods.name: next
methods.description: Moves the iterator to the next segment of the path forwards along the primary direction of traversal as long as there are more points in that direction.

methods.name: currentSegment
methods.description: Returns the coordinates and type of the current path segment in the iteration. The return value is the path segment type: SEG_MOVETO, SEG_LINETO, or SEG_CLOSE. A float array of length 6 must be passed in and can be used to store the coordinates of the point(s). Each point is stored as a pair of float x,y coordinates. SEG_MOVETO and SEG_LINETO types return one point, and SEG_CLOSE does not return any points.

parameters.name: coords
parameters.description: an array that holds the data returned from this method

throws.description: if there are no more elements in the flattening path to be returned.

methods.returns: the path segment type of the current path segment.

methods.name: currentSegment
methods.description: Returns the coordinates and type of the current path segment in the iteration. The return value is the path segment type: SEG_MOVETO, SEG_LINETO, or SEG_CLOSE. A double array of length 6 must be passed in and can be used to store the coordinates of the point(s). Each point is stored as a pair of double x,y coordinates. SEG_MOVETO and SEG_LINETO types return one point, and SEG_CLOSE does not return any points.

parameters.name: coords
parameters.description: an array that holds the data returned from this method

throws.description: if there are no more elements in the flattening path to be returned.

methods.returns: the path segment type of the current path segment.
